proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of the marginal treatment effect curve and policy-relevant treatment effects with two or more binary instruments, overlapping but distinct propensity-score supports, and a flexible MTE shape class (Bernstein-polynomial basis): a closed-form linear program characterizing the identified set as a function of the joint instrument-propensity distribution, with an explicit dual representation that yields PRTE bounds strictly tighter than the single-instrument MST (2018, Section 6) benchmark by a computable amount equal to the moment-cone width of the joint propensity distribution projected onto the basis; recovers Mogstad-Santos-Torgovitsky (2018, ECMA) Theorem 1 in the single-instrument special case. MST (2018, Section 6, p. 1623) explicitly state the multi-instrument extension is left for future work.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue artifacts remain pid_dose_response_iv/v1 and pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers/v1, which are adjacent IV partial-identification entries but not multi-binary-IV Bernstein MTE proposals and do not analyze bounded MTW partial-monotonicity response-cell violations. The downgraded pid_iv_bounded_defier_envelope/v1 and pid_dynamic_iv_compliance/v1 warn against generic finite-type LP duality; the retained gap is the active-cell PRTE sensitivity map under a sharp violation budget.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2024 for exact partial-monotonicity multi-IV MTE, JiangSun2023 for testing partial instrument monotonicity, and SunWuthrich2025 for pairwise valid instruments under partially invalid pairs. The flagship epsilon-gap is that none parameterizes a bounded mass of MTW response-cell monotonicity violations and derives endpoint-specific Bernstein-PRTE support-price derivatives or a zero-sensitivity diagnostic."}
seed_list: Prior work MogstadSantosTorgovitsky2018 would predict that IV-like Bernstein moments bound PRTEs through the instrument at hand. Our seed conjectures a sharper joint-IV bound whose improvement over the best single-IV MST interval equals a projected moment-cone width, because overlapping binary-IV propensity supports create quotient-cone directions invisible to every single coarsening.

Prior work HanYang2024 has established sharp binary-instrument LP bounds for policy-relevant MTE averages. Our seed conjectures that the active dual certificate itself decomposes by instrument-pair support chords, because distinct binary instruments can tighten different Bernstein basis directions even when no single instrument dominates.

Prior work MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2024 would imply that multiple instruments can be aggregated through mutual consistency. Our seed conjectures a basis-rank phase transition for PRTE span, because the target policy weight enters the joint Bernstein cone only after enough non-nested propensity chords are present.

Prior work Goff2024 has established LATE-type identification under vector monotonicity for multiple binary instruments. Our seed conjectures that vector-monotone LATE point identification and PRTE support equivalence separate, because LATE groups can be identified while the PRTE Bernstein weight remains outside the generated cone.

Prior work MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2021 has shown full multi-IV monotonicity is nearly homogeneous and motivates partial monotonicity. Our seed conjectures bounded partial-monotonicity violations expand multi-IV PRTE bounds by a kinked dual sensitivity term, because small defier budgets first relax only the active support faces.

Prior work SasakiUra2023 has established orthogonal-score PRTE inference for smooth PRTE estimands. Our seed conjectures inference for the multi-IV tightening gain is nonsmooth at slack joint moments and tied single-IV benchmarks, because the gain is a difference of LP support functions.

Prior work AndrewsRothPakes2023 would suggest conditional moment-inequality tools for inference in linear systems. Our seed conjectures a specialized bootstrap for the projected width gain, because generic moment-inequality confidence sets do not isolate the scalar support-function difference.

Prior work HeckmanVytlacil2005 would predict that policy relevance depends on whether IV variation spans the policy-affected margin. Our seed conjectures a necessary-and-sufficient Bernstein cone condition for exact PRTE support equivalence, because overlapping binary supports can add moments without spanning the policy weight.

Prior work MogstadTorgovitsky2018Review assumes extrapolation from instrument-affected to policy-affected individuals is the central PRTE difficulty. Our seed conjectures that multi-IV extrapolation difficulty is exactly a residual Bernstein-weight norm after projection on joint propensity chords, because the residual determines the remaining partial-ID width.

Prior work MourifieWan2025 has shown imperfect-IV layers can bound MTE/PRTE parameters. Our seed conjectures valid multiple IVs plus a bounded violation budget interpolate between joint-support MST bounds and imperfect-IV bounds, because the violation budget acts as an l1 relaxation of instrument-specific moment rows.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MogstadSantosTorgovitsky2018 would predict that IV-like Bernstein moments bound PRTEs through the instrument at hand. Our seed conjectures a sharper joint-IV bound whose improvement over the best single-IV MST interval equals a projected moment-cone width, because overlapping binary-IV propensity supports create quotient-cone directions invisible to every single coarsening.

anchor_paper.author: Magne Mogstad, Andres Santos, Alexander Torgovitsky
anchor_paper.claim: MST express IV-like estimands and policy parameters as weighted averages of MTE/MTR functions and use Bernstein-polynomial LP bounds; their Section 6 future-work discussion leaves the multi-instrument extension open.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift; not blocked because the extension is required to produce a new computable projected-cone gain term and strictness criterion, not merely more instruments in the same LP.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from MogstadSantosTorgovitsky2018 because adding IV-like rows to an LP and dualizing is routine.
seed_details.rebuttal: Generic LP duality gives nested bounds, but not the exact best-single-IV comparison, quotient-cone width, or strict-improvement condition as a function of the joint propensity distribution.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work HanYang2024 has established sharp binary-instrument LP bounds for policy-relevant MTE averages. Our seed conjectures that the active dual certificate itself decomposes by instrument-pair support chords, because distinct binary instruments can tighten different Bernstein basis directions even when no single instrument dominates.

anchor_paper.author: Sukjin Han, Shenshen Yang
anchor_paper.claim: Provides sharp nonparametric LP bounds for policy-relevant weighted averages of the MTE when instruments are binary, but does not give the projected moment-cone width comparison to a single-IV MST benchmark.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition; not blocked because it identifies active support-chord geometry absent from the published LP statement.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from HanYang2024 because their sharp LP already contains the binary-instrument constraints.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gap is not the primal LP but the interpretable dual decomposition and best-single-instrument improvement formula.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2024 would imply that multiple instruments can be aggregated through mutual consistency. Our seed conjectures a basis-rank phase transition for PRTE span, because the target policy weight enters the joint Bernstein cone only after enough non-nested propensity chords are present.

anchor_paper.author: Magne Mogstad, Alexander Torgovitsky, Christopher R. Walters
anchor_paper.claim: Develops a multiple-IV MTE framework under partial monotonicity and mutual-consistency constraints, showing that information from multiple instruments can be aggregated more informatively than using each instrument separately.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift; not blocked because the proposed rank threshold is a phase-transition claim about policy-weight span.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2024 because mutual consistency already states when multiple instruments aggregate information.
seed_details.rebuttal: MTW 2024 does not state a Bernstein-basis rank threshold or a PRTE target-cone entry condition relative to the MST benchmark.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Goff2024 has established LATE-type identification under vector monotonicity for multiple binary instruments. Our seed conjectures that vector-monotone LATE point identification and PRTE support equivalence separate, because LATE groups can be identified while the PRTE Bernstein weight remains outside the generated cone.

anchor_paper.author: Leonard Goff
anchor_paper.claim: For multiple binary instruments, vector monotonicity point-identifies LATE-type parameters for groups responsive to instruments or subsets of instruments, but not a full policy-weight support equivalence theorem for PRTE bounds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation; not blocked because the PRTE target reveals a support-equivalence gap hidden by LATE identification.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Goff2024 because PRTEs are just weighted averages of identified LATE groups.
seed_details.rebuttal: The policy weight need not lie in the cone generated by LATE group weights, so point-identified local effects do not imply PRTE support equivalence.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2021 has shown full multi-IV monotonicity is nearly homogeneous and motivates partial monotonicity. Our seed conjectures bounded partial-monotonicity violations expand multi-IV PRTE bounds by a kinked dual sensitivity term, because small defier budgets first relax only the active support faces.

anchor_paper.author: Magne Mogstad, Alexander Torgovitsky, Christopher R. Walters
anchor_paper.claim: Shows that standard monotonicity with multiple IVs is too restrictive unless choice behavior is effectively homogeneous, motivating partial monotonicity.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assumption weakening; not blocked because the weakening predicts a kinked dual sensitivity path, not just a larger feasible set.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from sensitivity analysis because relaxing monotonicity just adds slack variables to an LP.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed contribution is the active-face expansion formula for PRTE bounds under multi-IV partial-monotonicity violations.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work SasakiUra2023 has established orthogonal-score PRTE inference for smooth PRTE estimands. Our seed conjectures inference for the multi-IV tightening gain is nonsmooth at slack joint moments and tied single-IV benchmarks, because the gain is a difference of LP support functions.

anchor_paper.author: Yuya Sasaki, Takuya Ura
anchor_paper.claim: Develops PRTE inference with orthogonal scores and emphasizes preliminary nuisance estimates, but not LP-bound improvement functionals.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation; not blocked because the tightening gain is nonsmooth and can be degenerate, unlike smooth PRTE estimation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This follows from the delta method for SasakiUra2023 once the LP value is estimated.
seed_details.rebuttal: LP value differences fail ordinary differentiability at slack moments and ties among single-IV benchmarks, requiring a separate nonsmooth argument.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AndrewsRothPakes2023 would suggest conditional moment-inequality tools for inference in linear systems. Our seed conjectures a specialized bootstrap for the projected width gain, because generic moment-inequality confidence sets do not isolate the scalar support-function difference.

anchor_paper.author: Isaiah Andrews, Jonathan Roth, Ariel Pakes
anchor_paper.claim: Provides uniformly valid inference for conditional moment inequalities, a natural but not yet specialized tool for Bernstein-MTE LP support functions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends method; not blocked because the extension targets a degenerating scalar difference of support functions rather than a generic confidence set.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from AndrewsRothPakes2023 because the LP constraints are linear moment inequalities.
seed_details.rebuttal: Uniform inference for the identified set does not directly give valid inference for the gain over the minimum single-IV benchmark under ties and slack added moments.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work HeckmanVytlacil2005 would predict that policy relevance depends on whether IV variation spans the policy-affected margin. Our seed conjectures a necessary-and-sufficient Bernstein cone condition for exact PRTE support equivalence, because overlapping binary supports can add moments without spanning the policy weight.

anchor_paper.author: James J. Heckman, Edward Vytlacil
anchor_paper.claim: Establishes the MTE policy-evaluation framework and emphasizes that IV methods can fail to answer policy questions when heterogeneity enters both choices and outcomes.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Settled-but-unclear scope; not blocked because it gives a necessary-and-sufficient support condition for several binary instruments.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the MTE weight representation in HeckmanVytlacil2005.
seed_details.rebuttal: The representation shows what should be spanned, but not the finite binary-IV Bernstein cone condition or residual-bound formula.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MogstadTorgovitsky2018Review assumes extrapolation from instrument-affected to policy-affected individuals is the central PRTE difficulty. Our seed conjectures that multi-IV extrapolation difficulty is exactly a residual Bernstein-weight norm after projection on joint propensity chords, because the residual determines the remaining partial-ID width.

anchor_paper.author: Magne Mogstad, Alexander Torgovitsky
anchor_paper.claim: Reviews that policy relevance usually requires extrapolating from instrument-affected individuals to the population affected by the policy.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition; not blocked because it translates extrapolation difficulty into a finite-basis residual-width object.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a restatement of extrapolation from the Annual Review survey.
seed_details.rebuttal: The survey motivates extrapolation, while the seed proposes an algebraic residual norm tied to joint binary-IV support.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MourifieWan2025 has shown imperfect-IV layers can bound MTE/PRTE parameters. Our seed conjectures valid multiple IVs plus a bounded violation budget interpolate between joint-support MST bounds and imperfect-IV bounds, because the violation budget acts as an l1 relaxation of instrument-specific moment rows.

anchor_paper.author: Ismael Mourifie, Yuanyuan Wan
anchor_paper.claim: Derives sharp MTE/PRTE bounds under imperfect IV assumptions, showing how MTE policy analysis can be layered when full validity fails.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assumption weakening; not blocked because it predicts an interpolation path and dual l1 relaxation rather than merely allowing invalid IVs.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from MourifieWan2025 because imperfect IV assumptions already relax validity.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed is specific to multiple valid-IV support aggregation plus a bounded violation budget over instrument-specific moment rows.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Heckman-Vytlacil 2005 Econometrica and Vytlacil 2002 Econometrica anchor the latent-index/MTE equivalence and policy-evaluation setup.
Manski-Pepper 2000 Econometrica supplies the monotone-IV/MTR partial-ID tradition, useful as a nonparametric-bounds ancestor.
Mogstad-Santos-Torgovitsky 2018 Econometrica is the direct single-instrument MST benchmark: IV-like moments, shape restrictions, Bernstein-polynomial computation, LP bounds.
MST's supplement discusses Bernstein-polynomial computational advantages for the finite-dimensional LP.
Mogstad-Torgovitsky-Walters 2021 AER identifies the core multiple-IV monotonicity problem: standard monotonicity is too restrictive with multiple distinct IVs.
Mogstad-Torgovitsky-Walters 2024 Journal of Econometrics is the closest hit: multi-IV MTE under partial monotonicity and mutual consistency.
Goff 2024 Journal of Econometrics gives a binary multi-IV vector-monotonicity LATE identification theory, adjacent but not PRTE/MTE partial-ID LP.
Han-Yang 2024 Journal of Econometrics gives sharp LP bounds for policy-relevant MTE averages with binary instruments, adjacent to the requested LP formulation.
Marx 2024 Journal of Econometrics gives analytic sharp bounds for latent-index MTE functionals using full marginal-distribution content.
Kitagawa 2021 Journal of Econometrics characterizes sharp IV identification regions under instrument independence, useful for exact-ID-region logic.
Bartalotti-Kedagni-Possebom 2023 Journal of Econometrics extends sharp MTE bounds to sample selection and discrete instruments.
Sasaki-Ura 2023 Journal of Econometrics handles PRTE estimation/inference with orthogonal scores, relevant after identification.
Mourifie-Wan 2025 Journal of Econometrics extends MTE/PRTE bounds to imperfect IV layers, distinct from multiple valid IV support aggregation.
Jiang-Sun 2023 arXiv tests partial instrument monotonicity, a validity diagnostic for the multi-IV cluster.
Andrews-Roth-Pakes 2023 Review of Economic Studies supplies modern inference tools for linear conditional moment inequalities likely needed for LP-bound inference.
